audience: Roo user who wants memory portability first
evidence: Official Roo docs now carry a hard shutdown date and point users to Cline, while the hosted migration guide keeps the differentiator on portable lessons instead of generic replacement hype.
proofTrigger: They can name one correction, rule, or blocked command they do not want to re-teach after the migration.
recommendedMotion: Migration guide -> setup guide -> Pro once one blocked repeat is real.

audience: Cline evaluator who wants the shortest proof-backed install path
evidence: The guide and adapter install doc already show the exact `npx thumbgate init --agent cline` path, the local `.thumbgate/memory.sqlite` store, and the pre-action gate layer.
proofTrigger: They want to inspect the exact setup files or validate the install without widening the pitch into a services motion.
recommendedMotion: Migration guide -> exact install doc -> Pro only after proof of one blocked repeat.

audience: Workflow owner facing migration plus rollout risk
evidence: Roo sunset urgency creates a natural review point for teams that already have one approval-boundary or rollback-risk workflow they cannot afford to reset badly.
proofTrigger: They already named one risky workflow or one production-step repeat they need gated before the migration is considered safe.
recommendedMotion: Qualify one workflow for the Workflow Hardening Sprint before discussing broader rollout.
